description: Math-first content: equations, proofs, derivations, worked problems, symbolic notation, trigonometry, calculus, statistics, geometry, or measurement problems where the main function is mathematical reasoning.
Programming or software-command content with code snippets, API calls, command syntax, stack traces, configuration, or developer documentation as the main format.
Instructional or explanatory material intended to teach a learner: lesson plans, classroom activities, student-facing explainers, tutorials, study notes, and guided learning resources.

Reference-like informative text: neutral descriptions, book/resource entries, patent-style descriptions, catalog records, biographies, historical summaries, and factual background pages not primarily written as lessons.
Wiki-like or encyclopedia-derived reference content with compact neutral summaries, article stubs, license/citation boilerplate, or explicit Wikipedia-style provenance.
examples: A Science Fair Project Encyclopedia page

description: General web article or blog-like prose that is coherent but not clearly educational, reference, news, forum, product, legal, code, math, or boilerplate.
News-like reporting about events, studies, accidents, politics, announcements, public developments, or recent updates, usually with reported facts and attribution.
Question-and-answer or discussion-thread content with user questions, answers, comments, replies, homework help, troubleshooting, or conversational exchanges.
Commercial product or service page: sales copy, product features, offers, prices, purchase calls to action, warranties, stock status, and retail descriptions.
negative_keywords: technical reference with no purchase intent

description: Mostly low-content, generated, spammy, navigational, legal/footer, broken, repeated, or SEO-like text where useful primary content is weak or absent.
negative_keywords: coherent article with only small boilerplate

description: Government, legal, public policy, official notice, regulation, public registry, hearing, compliance, or civic administrative information.
Fallback when the document genre or format is unclear, too mixed, too short, or not confidently represented by the other source_type labels.
